column1: #   id        short label printed in the summary table
#   severity  one of: crit | high | mid | low
#   regex     Rust-regex (ripgrep) pattern; use \b for word boundaries
#   note      one-line replacement / rationale, printed in the report
# Lines starting with '#' and blank lines are ignored. Rows with fewer than
# 4 non-empty TAB-separated fields, or with an unknown severity, emit a
# warning AND make the run exit 2 — they are treated as bad rules because a
# silently disabled rule leaves a coverage gap CI cannot detect.
# To add a rule, append a new TAB-separated line. No code change needed.
#   crit  removed by current standard; appearance is a compile-time bug
#   high  active footgun with a known-better replacement (function/regex/list/async)
#   mid   structural smell that needs case-by-case judgement (map/set/vector<bool>)
#   low   acceptable but worth knowing (deque / iostream output)
# --- crit: should never appear in C++17+ code ---

column4: Removed in C++17. Use std::unique_ptr.
Removed in C++17. Use std::shuffle with a real engine.
C++11 GC interface, removed in C++23 and never implemented (incl. the std::pointer_safety enum).
Removed in C++17. Use lambdas.

Empty function-trailer `throw()` deprecated in C++17 (== noexcept(true)), removed in C++20 (incl. ref-qualified `& throw()` / `&& throw()` and cv-qualified forms). Use noexcept. Promote to crit when the project targets C++20+. (Non-empty `throw(X)` removed in C++17 itself, but is not regexed here to avoid false positives on `throw (T)` expressions; the C++17 compiler catches it.)
Trigraphs removed in C++17.
C gets() removed in C11/C++14 (incl. ::gets and std::gets). Use fgets / std::getline.
const op() invokes non-const callable. Prefer template parameter, tl::function_ref (C++17), or std::move_only_function (C++23).
std::regex is documented-slow (P1844R1). Use CTRE (compile-time pattern), RE2 (user input), or Boost.Regex (general).
Almost always wrong. Use std::vector (default), std::deque (two-ended), or Boost.Intrusive (true intrusive list).
Returned future blocks in destructor (deadlock trap). Use a thread pool or std::thread directly.
Standard explicitly permits implementations to be deterministic (libstdc++ MinGW shipped this way for years). Verify entropy on target platform; test-only seeding is usually fine.
Expression-template optimization never implemented. Use Eigen / xtensor / Blaze.
Deprecated in C++23 (incl. _t alias templates). Use alignas(T) std::byte[sizeof(T)] directly.

std::iterator base class deprecated in C++17. Requires whitespace before `,` or `{` so brace-init member-initializer forms like `X() : std::iterator<...>{}` are not flagged. Misses inheritance with no space before `{`/`,` (uncommon style) and nested templates in std::iterator's args. Define the five iterator typedefs yourself.
Deprecated in C++17, removed in C++20. Use std::not_fn or lambda.

Proxy semantics break generic code expecting std::vector<T>& (incl. allocator-template form `std::vector<bool, Alloc>` and std::pmr::vector<bool>). Use std::vector<std::uint8_t> or boost::dynamic_bitset.
Red-black tree, node-per-element. Prefer absl::btree_map or boost::container::flat_map. If keys are dense ints, use std::vector.
Same as std::map.
Standard's bucket interface and reference-stability rules effectively rule out open addressing. Use absl::flat_hash_map, boost::unordered_flat_map, or ankerl::unordered_dense.
Same as std::unordered_map.
Deprecated in C++17, removed in C++20. Use std::vector or std::uninitialized_*.
Deprecated in C++17, removed in C++20 (incl. _t alias). Use std::invoke_result.
Deprecated in C++20 (incl. _v variable template). Use std::is_trivially_copyable && std::is_standard_layout.
Single-form deprecated in C++17, removed in C++20 (incl. function-pointer references). Use std::uncaught_exceptions().
TS namespace; migrate to the standardized C++17/20/23 equivalent.

register removed as storage-class in C++17. Demoted to mid because the English word "register" generates comment / docstring false positives. Type list intentionally restricted to primitives and `std::`-prefixed types; `register UserType x;` is missed because the user-type form is uncommon and a permissive pattern re-introduces comment FPs.
Deprecated since C++98 (still in the standard, never removed). Use std::stringstream.
Acceptable for two-ended FIFO. MSVC STL has known cache-unfriendly block size (microsoft/STL#147).
Slow, locale-bound, thread-unsafe formatting. Prefer fmt::print (C++17) or std::print (C++23). Acceptable behind a verbose flag.
